findings.evidence: `RamificationInertia.lean:33` declares the theorem under `namespace TauCeti.DedekindDomain`; lines 34-36 show `[IsDomain A] [IsDomain B] [Module.Flat A B]` and no `IsDedekindDomain` assumption.
findings.fix: Move the generalized theorem to a neutral namespace/file such as `TauCeti.RamificationInertia` or keep the public Dedekind-domain name only for the compatibility wrapper and give the generalized theorem a namespace/name matching domain/flat assumptions.
findings.issue: `TauCeti.DedekindDomain.ncard_primesOver_eq_natCard_iff_of_isGaloisGroup_of_isDomain` is in the `DedekindDomain` namespace/file but its signature assumes only `[IsDomain A] [IsDomain B] [Module.Flat A B]`, so the namespace advertises a stronger and different context than the theorem actually has.

summary: The new public theorem is named and namespaced as Dedekind-domain material even though its signature is only domain/flat. That makes the API misleading from the naming angle.
